lecture_summary: The lecture covers the concept of depreciation, focusing on the straight-line method. It includes calculating annual depreciation, determining the original cost, and applying these calculations to a specific example involving a ship.

concepts.name: Depreciation
concepts.description: Depreciation is the reduction in the value of an asset over time, calculated using methods such as the straight-line method.
key_points: The straight-line method results in equal depreciation each year.

concepts.name: Straight-Line Method of Depreciation
concepts.description: A method of calculating depreciation where the asset loses the same amount of value each year.
key_points: Depreciation amount is constant each year.

concepts.name: Annual Depreciation Calculation
concepts.description: The process of determining the depreciation expense for a single year using the straight-line method.
key_points: Depreciation rate is applied to the original cost.

concepts.name: Original Cost
concepts.description: The initial value of an asset before any depreciation is applied.
key_points: Original cost is necessary for calculating depreciation.
Example: Calculated as ₹15,00,000 from given depreciated value.

concepts.name: Depreciated Value
concepts.description: The value of an asset after accounting for depreciation.
key_points: Depreciated value is the original cost minus accumulated depreciation.

concepts.name: Depreciation Rate
concepts.description: The percentage of the asset's value that is depreciated each year.
key_points: 12% per annum in the given example.

concepts.name: Time Period for Depreciation
concepts.description: The duration over which depreciation is calculated.
key_points: Depreciation calculated for specific periods, e.g., 146 days.
Pro-rated based on the number of days in a year.

analogies_used.analogy: Depreciation is like subtracting ₹12 each year from an asset worth ₹100.
